title: Enhancing Learning Mechanisms in Cognitive Architectures Using Hidden Markov Models
abstract: This paper explores the integration of Hidden Markov Models (HMMs) into cognitive architectures to enhance learning mechanisms. Building on the foundational work presented in 'Blocking Gibbs Sampling for Linkage Analysis in Large Pedigrees with Many Loops,' we investigate how HMMs can be leveraged to combine inductive and analytical learning methods. We propose a novel framework, HMM-EBNN, which integrates HMMs with Explanation Based Neural Network learning (EBNN) to improve the learning of complex tasks. Our experimental results demonstrate significant improvements in learning control strategies for a mobile robot, showcasing the potential of HMMs in enhancing general-purpose learning mechanisms. The study also addresses convergence diagnostics and Bayesian inference, drawing insights from related works on MCMC algorithms and exact sampling methods.
